events.event_summary: Trump confirmed that Apple has agreed to have Intel manufacture chips for it, joining previously announced Nvidia and Tesla deals under a US manufacturing push.
primary_drivers: Foundry market share shift from TSMC to Intel Foundry Services
US onshoring capex pull-forward for advanced node tools
Validation of Intel 18A/14A as a viable leading-edge foundry for hyperscalers/fabless

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest WFE vendor; Intel ramping 18A/14A capacity for Apple/NVDA/TSLA wafers requires incremental deposition/etch/CMP tools, plus US fab buildout drives sustained capex orders.
Etch/deposition leader with strong exposure to Intel's logic ramps; new foundry customer wins increase wafer starts and tool pull at Arizona/Ohio fabs.
Process control intensity is highest at leading-edge nodes; Intel taking on multiple external customers at 18A increases yield-learning urgency and KLA tool buys.
EUV/High-NA dependency for Intel 18A and 14A ramps; more foundry customers = more wafer demand = more EUV scanner orders, though ASML already books most Intel slots.
Cleaning/plating equipment supplier leveraged to incremental US fab buildout and capacity expansion at Intel.

losers.rationale: Apple is TSMC's #1 customer (~25% of revenue) on N3/N2; any shift of Apple silicon wafers to Intel Foundry directly cannibalizes TSMC's leading-edge mix and pricing power.
GlobalFoundries' pitch as the US-onshored foundry alternative is undermined if Intel becomes the de facto US foundry for marquee customers, even though GFS plays at trailing nodes.
Taiwan foundry peer that re-rates negatively on any geopolitical/onshoring wafer reallocation theme away from Taiwan.

headlines: After Nvidia And Tesla, Trump Confirms Apple Has Agreed To Build Chips With Intel Amid US Manufacturing Push: 'When Was The Last Time...'
